title: DNS Gateway Configuration
description: Configuration for the legacy combined dns-gateway extension. Pass with --filter-type udp_listener for the resolver filter (see dns-gateway/resolver), or --filter-type network for the lookup filter (see dns-gateway/lookup).

domains.description: Domain matchers, each with its own CIDR range for virtual IP allocation.

domain.description: Exact domain ("example.com") or single-level wildcard pattern ("*.example.com").

base_ip.description: Base address for virtual IP allocation. May be IPv4 (e.g. "10.0.0.0", answered as an A record) or IPv6 (e.g. "fd00::", answered as an AAAA record). The address family of base_ip determines which DNS record type this domain answers.

anyOf.description: IPv4 base address (answered as an A record).
IPv6 base address (answered as an AAAA record).

prefix_len.description: CIDR prefix length. For an IPv4 base_ip the valid range is 1-32 (a /24 gives 256 virtual IPs); for an IPv6 base_ip it is 1-128 (a /64 gives 2^64 virtual IPs). Prefixes beyond the base_ip's family bound are rejected at config load.

metadata.description: String key-value pairs exposed in Envoy filter state as io.builtonenvoy.dns_gateway.metadata.\<key\>.

fail_open.description: If true, forward queries to upstream resolvers when a CIDR range is exhausted. Default: false (return NODATA, preventing matched domains from leaking to upstream resolvers).

filter_state_prefix.description: Prefix for filter state keys written by the lookup filter. Downstream filters read domain and metadata under this prefix. Default: "io.builtonenvoy.dns_gateway".
